entry.title: Re: Features & functions of app Cash Flow Analyzer (F2332) in S/4HANA Cash and Liquidity Managem
entry.content:
Thx

One of the customers ask me if this App can be modified with adding required fields to the simulation view in the cash flow Analyzer Fiori App.
This functionality is critical for the customer’s daily operations.
 When accessing the cash concentration simulation view, we’re unable to add any additional fields to the report.
This capability is essential for customers, as they rely on these fields, such as the company code, when planning their daily cash pools.

Thx

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite issue with Deep Entity create: depe...
entry.content:
Well I solved by Myself as this looks like a limitation of XML to JSON converter, the items were getting themselves a new group tag <Entregas> for example, which in turn made the mechanism that processes the payload in the OData connector to read only the last group, instead of everything being under a single <Entregas>.

After checking note 2478282 and the XML to JSON Converter in INtegration Suite docs, i realized that this was a limitation of the tool. This might be solvable using a message mapping but at least for me it was easier to create a Groovy Script to do the parsing correctly, and it worked like a charm.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: The SSAM app performs a full sync instead of a delta sync on login after 1–3weeks of inactivity
entry.content:
Issue Summary

When users of SAP Service and Asset Manager (SSAM 2405) do not use the mobile application for 1–3 weeks, the next login triggers a full data synchronization instead of a delta sync, resulting in significantly longer sync times.

Detailed Description

  • Users have reported that after a period of inactivity (approximately 2–3 weeks), logging into the SSAM mobile application triggers a full synchronization.

  • Normally, the application performs a delta sync to update only changed records, which is faster and more efficient.

  • Due to the full sync, the login process takes an extended time, impacting productivity and delaying field operations.

  • This behavior is observed consistently across multiple devices and users who have been idle for an extended period.

Expected Behavior

  • After a period of inactivity, logging into SSAM should perform a delta sync (syncing only changed data), not a full sync, unless there are configuration or data inconsistencies requiring a full sync.

  • The sync time should remain comparable to normal delta sync durations.

Actual Behavior

  • After 1–3 weeks of inactivity, the application performs a full sync, which significantly increases login and data retrieval time.

Impact

  • Reduced user productivity due to long sync times.

  • Delays in field operations and work order processing.

  • Increased network and backend load during full sync events.

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Do not log in to the SSAM mobile application for 2–3 weeks.

  2. Open the SSAM mobile app and log in.

  3. Observe that the application performs a full sync instead of a delta sync.

  4. Note the extended sync duration compared to normal delta sync operations.

Request / Questions for SAP Support

  • Is this behavior expected for extended idle periods, or is it a potential issue with delta sync?

  • Are there configuration settings or best practices to ensure that the application performs a delta sync even after long periods of inactivity?

  • Is there a recommended way to minimize sync time for users returning after prolonged inactivity?

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BAS) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - error 4
entry.content:
This note helped me: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3679283

Using S4HC tracing, it turned out that it was adding a "/" to the end of my Destination Audience URL. 

Works: https://my234567.s4hana.cloud.sap

Works not: https://my234567.s4hana.cloud.sap/

entry.title: Re: ADT HTTP Service created with error
entry.content:
@saurabh__khare38 any news on this? I ran into the same issue today.

BR Chris

entry.title: Re: ADT HTTP Service created with error
entry.content:
Hi Chris,

We raised an incident to SAP. They activated/re-activated the repository object type HTTP in SE80. This will ensure that the discovery is correctly generated. That resolved the issue.

Case for your reference 1446133/2025 

Also, note that for services created using ADT no SICF node is created. This is the new behavior from S/4H 2022 onwards. You need to activate the generated service via UCON_HTTP_SERVICES on GUI. This works irrespective of whether the service details are visible in the ADT editor or not.

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI: March 12, 2026
entry.content:
Managing incoming documents efficiently is critical for business operations. SAP Cloud ERP, powered by SAP Document AI, offers pre-delivered use cases to automate and optimize document processing across Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.

In this live session, we’ll explore how SAP Document AI enhances document inbound processing, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy. You’ll see live demos of end-to-end workflows and learn how to activate these capabilities in your system.

Key Takeaways:

  • Discover pre-built document processing use cases in SAP Cloud ERP.
  • See live demos of end-to-end workflows for Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.
  • Learn how to activate and extend these capabilities for your business needs.

Who Should Attend? Project managers, consultants, business users, and administrators looking to improve document processing efficiency.

Register Now!

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026
entry.content:
Hello Team, I hope you all are doing well!

I prepared for the certification exam - SAP Certified Associate - Integration Developer. It shows " This SAP Certification Exam will be replaced with a practical exam very soon." since many weeks now. 

Can you please inform when the practical exam will be available? I have a fear of losing my knowledge and hand-on practice preparation if it is not going to be available soon. Thank you!

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA: Installation number "INITIAL"
entry.content:
Hi there!

My SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA running on azure should have the installation number "DEMOSYSTEM" but instead the installation number is "INITIAL".

Screenshot from System info:

Screenshot from SLICENSE:

 

 

With this configuration it is not possible to install ABAP Open Source Namespaces.

What am I doing wrong?

@sebastian_wolf

entry.title: The Future of Intelligent Hiring with SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors (Americas)
entry.content:
Hiring is evolving faster than ever – and we’re quickly moving toward a future that’s more intelligent, data-driven, and people-centric. To stay competitive, future-ready organizations must place insights, AI, and skills at the center of their talent strategies to make smarter hiring decisions and drive better business outcomes.

Join this session to learn how intelligent hiring helps organizations elevate talent experiences, accelerate hiring processes, and ensure every hire is aligned with business and workforce goals. Discover how SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors works alongside the SAP SuccessFactors Learning and Talent portfolio to fuel speed, quality, and business success – empowering your organization to build a workforce ready for what’s next.

Key topics include:

•    Emerging trends in talent acquisition, insights on how AI is transforming hiring, and what this means for your organization

•    SAP’s strategic AI and product investments that enable organizations to make smarter hiring and business decisions

•    Live demo showcasing AI-driven innovations in SmartRecruiters to attract, hire, and retain the best talent

Register now to experience the future of intelligent hiring – and discover how your organization can attract top talent faster, engage the best candidates, and hire the skills needed to stay ahead.

Register now

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA: Installation number "INITIAL"
entry.content:
I had to install a new license although the current license was valid.

https://go.support.sap.com/minisap/#/minisap

Now the system is set to DEMOSYSTEM

entry.title: Re: Error while sending email using custom email template using ABAP program.
entry.content: Have you solved the issue? I assume so, as it seems easy if you know the values of IT_DATA_KEY and IR_DATA of the terminated method CL_SMTG_EMAIL_RENDERER -> APPLY (those are visible in the dump).

entry.title: Re: Removing the select all icon from ALV
entry.content: These are two workarounds which both have big drawbacks! (Either remove the column headers or remove the row selection marks). There's no solution to disable this button.

entry.title: Re: Discovery Package: Test, Demo, and Development for SAP Cloud ERP Private
entry.content: What are the SAP SLA's here for various activities.

entry.title: CL_GUI_ALV_GRID "select all" at grid top-left doesn't select all lines
entry.content:
The question was already asked here but was not answered.

I have a very simple editable ALV grid via CL_GUI_ALV_GRID, I have 3000 rows.

If I select all lines via the button "Select All" in the top left part of the grid (it's not in the toolbar, it's at the left of the column headers, above the column to select the rows), and I then click the button Delete in the toolbar to delete the lines, only around 300 rows are deleted.

So I have to repeat that 10 times to delete all rows.

What's the problem, how to select all rows?

My system: ABAP 7.58 SP 1.

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: CL_GUI_ALV_GRID "select all" at grid top-left doesn't select all lines
entry.content:
There's no standard solution.

One workaround is to add a custom button &ALL in the toolbar, it will select all rows as expected.

There's no way to hide the "Select All" button at the top left part of the grid (question already asked in the forum, for instance here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/removing-the-select-all-icon-from-alv/qaa-p/858449).

entry.title: Re: Adding 'Equals' Option to Selection Field in Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi @Mohamed_Mukhtar,

The available filter operators are determined by the field's data type and whether it has a value help - not by @consumption.filter.

The "equal to" operator appears when the field has a value help associated with it. Add a value help annotation to your field:
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { name: 'I_YourValueHelp', element: 'YourField' } }]

The field in your example that shows "equal to" likely has a value help bound to it.

entry.title: Re: Define routes of app router to ui5 landing page
entry.content:
Hi @YABAPER,

The managed approuter automatically serves content from inside the webapp folder. Your URL /baseui/webapp/index.html is effectively resolving to app/baseui/webapp/webapp/index.html, which doesn't exist.

Try /baseui/index.html instead.

entry.title: Configuring Job Status: Completed with Non‑Zero Return Code
entry.content:
I have a report that, when executed in the background, displays an error message if no data is returned, and the Job appears with a Canceled status in red. The issue is that whenever the Job status returns a value different from 0, it triggers another necessary process. However, the user does not want the status to be shown as Canceled when no data is found — they only want the return value to be different from 0.

My question is: Is it possible to configure the Job so that it is marked as Completed while still returning a status different from 0?

entry.title: Re: Configuring Job Status: Completed with Non‑Zero Return Code
entry.content:
There's no job status "0" in ABAP. Do you mean "0" in an external scheduler? I'm afraid the solution is to be checked by an expert of this external scheduler.

The job status Canceled is when an error message is sent with MESSAGE TYPE 'E', 'A' or 'X' by the program of the background job. You may end the job normally by not sending such an error message.

entry.title: Re: Sap BTP innovation
entry.content:
Thanks for taking the time to share such a thoughtful response — really appreciate the way you broke it down.

On your points:

1. BTP as a strategic platform Fully agree. In the utility programs I support, the shift has been from “BTP as an integration toolbox” to “BTP as the innovation and orchestration layer.” The decoupled innovation angle you mentioned is exactly what’s enabling utilities to modernize without destabilizing IS‑U or S/4HANA. Clean‑core is no longer a slogan — it’s survival.

2. Which BTP capability delivers the most value Spot on that each capability has its place. What I’m seeing in the field is that Integration Suite + Event Mesh is becoming the backbone for real‑time operations, while CAP is where teams are building lightweight, domain‑specific apps that would have been custom code inside IS‑U a few years ago. AI services are still early, but the direction is clear — especially with Joule agents maturing.

3. Preventing BTP from becoming the new custom‑code dumping ground Couldn’t agree more. This is 100% a governance and architecture discipline issue. In fact, many utilities are now creating BTP guardrails: extension review boards naming and packaging standards mandatory feasibility checks clean‑core enforcement policies lifecycle ownership defined upfront Without this, BTP can absolutely become “ECC 2.0” — just in the cloud. Thanks again for adding to the discussion — it’s great to see more practitioners sharing real‑world perspectives. These conversations are exactly what the SAP Community needs as we all navigate the shift toward AI‑driven architectures.

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing integration in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Alexander! Greetings to Heidelberg!

It’s great to see you working on such a cutting-edge integration between S/4HANA 1809 and SAP Subscription Billing. Transitioning to a "Clean Core" while maintaining legacy stability is a challenge many utility architects are currently navigating.

To address your questions directly: Yes, SAP CI (Convergent Invoicing) can definitely be integrated with S/4HANA On-Premise 1809, but the configuration path differs slightly from the Cloud version described in Chapter 4.2 of your guide.

1. Is there a difference in SAP CI Integration?

The primary difference is the Middleware and Interface strategy:

  • S/4HANA Cloud: Often uses pre-packaged integration content (Scope Item 5IK) via SAP Integration Suite (BTP), where Billable Items (BITs) are pushed directly into a managed Cloud API.

  • S/4HANA On-Premise 1809: You have more control but must manually configure the Billable Item Classes and the RFC or Web Service endpoints. While Cloud integration focuses on "Subscription Management with Sales Billing," your On-Premise setup allows for a tighter coupling with FI-CA (Utilities).

2. The Integration Path for S/4HANA 1809 (On-Premise)

Since you are using Release 1809, you should look at the SAP BRIM (Billing and Revenue Innovation Management) framework for On-Premise. Even if the guide you are following seems focused on Sales Billing (SD), the underlying mechanism for CI is the same.

Key Implementation Steps:

  1. BIT Class Creation: Use transaction FKKBIXBIT_CONF to create your Billable Item Classes. For Utilities, ensure you include interface components like Basic Data for Billing and Contract Reference.

  2. Middleware Setup: Use SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) to bridge SAP Subscription Billing to your S/4HANA On-Premise system. You will likely use the SOAMANAGER to configure the SOAP/Web Service endpoints that receive the bills and convert them into BITs.

  3. Utilities Coexistence: Since you mentioned FI-CA (Utilities), remember that IS-U and CI can coexist. You can run IS-U for commodity billing and use CI for non-commodity subscriptions (like EV charging or smart home services), consolidating both into a single FI-CA document for the customer.

Describing the Integration of SAP Subscription Billing and SAP Convergent Invoicing

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gJyG4uZ2vYw&start=7

Try this,.

entry.title: Re: LMS Onboarding Assignment Profile Rule
entry.content: Thank you, Kostas!

entry.title: Re: Report Recurring Pay - Multiple rows in Story Report
entry.content: Any luck with this one? I am having the same problem.

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
Struggling to Get the New SECURE 2.0 Catch‑Up Note Working in SAP Payroll (Section 603)

I’ve been working through the implementation of SAP’s new solution for SECURE 2.0 Act – Section 603 catch‑up contributions, and so far… I’m having a tough time getting the delivered logic to actually fire the way it’s supposed to.

I wanted to document where I’m stuck in case someone else runs into the same roadblocks — or, hopefully, someone can point out what I’m missing.

My Current Configuration

To test the rollover behavior from pre‑tax catch‑up to post‑tax catch‑up, here’s what I’ve set up:

V_T7USBEN03 (Catch‑Up Contribution Settings)

  • EE Catch‑up Contrib: BC31
  • EE CU 603 Rollover WT: BC32

(Yes, I know BC31/BC32 are just models — I used them “as‑is” to isolate whether the logic even triggers.)

V_T5F99K2 (BN603 Parameter Option)

  • Set to: WRNG_ROLL
    • This should cause P0169 to issue a warning and roll the unused pre‑tax catch‑up into the post‑tax wage type.

Employee Scenario for Testing

  • Employee whose prior‑year FICA wages exceed the 150k threshold for Section 602/603 rules
  • Should be forced into post‑tax catch‑up
  • Should produce the rollover wage type (BC32) during payroll

What Is Working

I did manage to get wage type /TB7 to generate during payroll.
So at least part of the Secure 2.0 logic is firing.

What Is Not Working

❌ BC32 never gets created

Despite the employee clearly meeting the compensation threshold, payroll never produces the rollover wage type.

❌ /TB6 never generates

This looks like my root issue.

According to SAP’s documentation, /TB6 is critical because it stores the prior‑year FICA wages — and if /TB6 has not exceeded the IRS compensation ceiling, no rollover will happen at all.

That means:

If /TB6 is missing, the system has nothing to compare against the BN603 threshold, so the rollover logic will never fire.

And that matches what I’m seeing.

Even though I ran payroll in the first period of the new year (when /TB6 is supposed to be created), the system only built /TB7, not /TB6.

So something about how /TB6 is generated — or not generated — is likely blocking the entire rollover flow.

Where I Think I’m Stuck

I feel like I’m missing one of these:

  • A prerequisite for generating /TB6
  • A required retro into the prior year
  • A missing TCRT value from the previous year
  • An issue with the first‑period run not recognizing the prior‑year results
  • A missing step in the payroll period generation or control record timing

Next Steps / What I’m Looking For

If anyone has successfully activated this note:

  • Did you have to trigger /TB6 manually somehow?
  • Did you have to run a retro into December?
  • Did payroll only pick up /TB6 when the prior year’s results existed in TCRT?
  • Were there additional steps needed beyond what the note describes?

At the moment, I’m convinced I’m close — but missing one key piece needed for /TB6 to appear so the full rollover logic can activate.

Any insight, tips, or “gotchas” would be hugely appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – New software delivery option “Add-On Product”
entry.content:
QQ  @Nora_Klemp 

1. How are partners balancing the operational responsibility shift when customers choose Add‑On Products instead of SaaS — especially around upgrade timing, dependency management, and ensuring compatibility with SAP’s quarterly BTP ABAP updates ?

2. For partners delivering Add‑On Products via SAP PE Build, what governance model does SAP recommend to avoid fragmentation across customer‑operated systems, especially when customers control patch schedules and extensibility?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Downtime-optimized Upgrades and Hotfix Collection Imports for Product
entry.content:
QQ @FrankJentsch 

1. With downtime‑optimized upgrades shifting most work to the preparation phase, how does SAP plan to improve predictability of the takeover timing so that customers can align business cutover windows more precisely, especially in regulated industries like utilities?

2. Since development systems are excluded from downtime‑optimized upgrades, how should partners and customers plan for consistency across dev/test/prod landscapes, especially when custom code volume influences takeover duration?”

3. For customers running partner Add‑On Products in their own BTP ABAP systems, how will downtime‑optimized upgrades interact with partner‑delivered software lifecycles, particularly when customers control patch timing but partners control component delivery?

1. How does the "Elastic Scaling" feature shift the ROI conversation for BTP ABAP Environment customers?

2. What are the specific architectural triggers that distinguish when a customer should choose "Elastic" over "Static" scaling?

3. How does this automated ACU scaling integrate with the broader "Clean Core" strategy?

entry.title: Re: Bring your own Git for SAP BTP ABAP Environment

QQ @christoph_pohl 

1. How does Git-based Lifecycle Management redefine the "Audit Trail" for regulated industries like Utilities?

2. What is the strategic significance of "Fingerprinting" in a multi-system, global account architecture?

3. In a "Clean Core" strategy, how does the choice of an external Git provider accelerate the Energy Transition for Utility firms?

entry.title: Re: Configuring Job Status: Completed with Non‑Zero Return Code
entry.content:
In the SAP background processing environment (SM37), the "Canceled" status is strictly triggered by unhandled Error (E), Abort (A), or Exit (X) messages.

If your goal is to have the job finish with a Finished status (Green) while still passing a specific return code to a subsequent process, you cannot rely on standard MESSAGE TYPE 'E'.

Here are the two most effective ways to achieve this:

1. The "Information/Warning" Workaround (Easiest)

Instead of throwing a hard error when no data is found, change the message type to 'I' (Information) or 'S' (Success) specifically for the background mode. This allows the job to complete normally in SM37 while your logic still records the "No Data" result.

IF itab_data[] IS INITIAL.
IF sy-batch = 'X'.
" In background: log as Info so the job stays 'Finished'
MESSAGE 'No data found' TYPE 'I'.
ELSE.
" In foreground: show as Error
MESSAGE 'No data found' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

" Set your specific return code here for the next process
gv_return_code = 4.
RETURN.
ENDIF.

2. Exporting the Return Code to Memory or Buffer

Since a "Finished" job always returns sy-subrc = 0 to the job scheduler, you need a different way to "hand off" your non-zero status to the next process:

  • SAP Memory/Database Table: Save your return code in a custom Z-table or use EXPORT TO DATABASE indexed by the Job ID. The next process can then read this value.

  • Job Log Analysis: The subsequent process can use the Function Module BP_JOBLOG_READ to check if the string "No data found" exists in the log. If it does, it treats it as a non-zero status even if the job is green.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Quantity API Tag
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am initiating machine integration with SAP Digital Manufacturing (DM). Previously, we developed a local MES where a machine’s PLC tag—for example, Total Sheets Produced—was updated every second. We captured the counter reading every five seconds to calculate instantaneous machine speed and map production progress.

Operators had an interface through which they would enter the production quantity for a specific production order. The system would then allocate the sheets produced to that order until the production order was changed, indicating the completion of production.

Additionally, when the counter incremented every five seconds, we marked the machine as in production. Conversely, when the counter stopped incrementing, we recorded the downtime start, and the downtime end was recorded when production resumed.

 How can this be catered in SAP DM. Any help would be greatly appreciated

Best regards,
Aziz Khan

SAP Digital Manufacturing 

@ManoelCosta

entry.title: Re: Double checks in one page from F110
entry.content: Hi Correct me if I'm wrong , you created a bank specific file format using DMEE and mapped same in the Payment medium tab, if client requirement is cheque you can use standard only -RFFOUS_C right ?

entry.title: How SAP Business AI Drives Sustainability in Automotive Manufacturing
entry.content:
Sustainability is no longer optional—it’s a business necessity. SAP Business AI helps companies integrate eco-friendly practices by analyzing environmental impact and suggesting sustainable alternatives.

Take Martur Fompak International (MFI), a leader in automotive seat production. MFI’s innovative solution uses SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to suggest sustainable fabric alternatives for seat covers. By analyzing their catalog with AI, MFI helps customers and internal teams compare materials based on carbon impact, enabling data-driven sustainability choices. Read the full story here.

Key benefits of SAP Business AI for sustainability include:

  • AI-driven recommendations for sustainable materials.
  • Carbon impact tracking to compare environmental footprints.
  • Seamless integration into business operations for long-term sustainability.

Summary 

  • SAP Business AI helps businesses analyze environmental impact and identify sustainable alternatives.
  • MFI uses AI to suggest eco-friendly fabrics and measure carbon footprints in automotive seat production.
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management provides data-driven insights for informed decision-making.
  • AI integration embeds sustainability into core business operations for long-term impact.

entry.title: Re: Header Condition in RFQ & Quotation - SAP Public Clou...
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if you have further questions.

entry.title: Prefilling a Value Help Filter from the Parent Entity in RAP
entry.content:
In a RAP application, I recently encountered a common requirement: pre‑filter a Value Help on a child entity field using a value coming from the parent entity (in my case, the CompanyCode from the header).

The scenario looks simple, but RAP currently cannot evaluate an association inside an additionalBinding. I tried the expected annotation:

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [
  {
    entity: { name : 'I_PurchaseOrderAPI01', element : 'PurchaseOrder' },
    additionalBinding: [
      { element: 'CompanyCode', localElement: '_Header.CompanyCode', usage: #FILTER }
    ]
  }
]
PurchaseOrder;
Unfortunately, the framework does not resolve the _Header association, and the filter is not applied.
There is an even more fundamental issue: When creating a child record, the child entity simply does not exist yet.  So you cannot push CompanyCode into the child entity and bind from there—there’s no child instance at that moment.
Because of this, annotations alone cannot solve the problem.
 

Workaround: Inject the Filter from a Fiori Elements Controller Extension

 The most pragmatic solution is to extend the Object Page controller and add the filter programmatically when the Value Help opens. This avoids backend limitations and works well during create flows.

Why my case is simpler

In my application, the PurchaseOrder field is a multi‑input on the header, not inside a table. That means:

  • There is one single generated field,
  • Easy to identify,
  • Easy to attach the opened event to its Value Help.

For tables, the logic is still possible but more dynamic (details below).

Implementation – Header Multi‑Input Scenario

Step 1 — Add a controller extension

In the Object Page, add a controller extension (via the page map in Fiori Tools).

 

The generated extension usually provides an onInit method override. Insert a call to your custom method:
onInit: function () {
  this._extendPurchaseOrderValueHelp();
}

Step 2 — Locate the generated field + attach a handler

Use Chrome DevTools to retrieve the generated ID for the multi‑input field. Then:

_extendPurchaseOrderVH: function () {

            const oView = this.base.getView();

            // 1) Get generated MDC field for PurchaseOrder
            const sPurchaseOrderId = "my.app::DocumentObjectPage--fe::FormContainer::FileTemplate::FormElement::DataField::_TemplatePurchaseOrder::PurchaseOrder::MultiValueField"; 
            let oField = oView.byId(sPurchaseOrderId);
            if (!oField) {
                return; // not rendered yet
            }

            // 2) Get MDC value help attach method to opened event
            const sPurchaseOrderValueHelpId = oField.getValueHelp && oField.getValueHelp(); // can return an id
            const oPurchaseOrderValueHelp = sPurchaseOrderValueHelpId ? oView.byId(sPurchaseOrderValueHelpId) : null;
            if (!oPurchaseOrderValueHelp) {
                return;
            }
            oPurchaseOrderValueHelp.attachOpened(this._onOpenPurchaseOrderValueHelp);

        }

This function:

  1. Retrieves the generated MDC field,
  2. Retrieves its Value Help,
  3. Hooks into the opened event.

Step 3 — Inject the parent’s CompanyCode into the Value Help

        _onOpenPurchaseOrderValueHelp: function (oEvent) {

            const oDocument = oEvent.getSource().getBindingContext().getObject();
            const oContainer = oEvent.getParameter("container");

            const aContents = oContainer.getContent ? oContainer.getContent() : [];
            aContents.forEach((oContent) => {
                if (oContent.getFilterBar) {
                    const oFilterBar = oContent.getFilterBar();
                    if (oFilterBar?.setFilterConditions) {
                        const mCond = oFilterBar.getFilterConditions ? oFilterBar.getFilterConditions() : {};
                        mCond.CompanyCode = [{
                            operator: "EQ",
                            values: [oDocument.CompanyCode]
                        }];
                        oFilterBar.setFilterConditions(mCond);
                    }
                }
            });

        }

A note about the table (item) scenario

In many applications, the Value Help is not in the header but inside a table row. This is actually the more common case.

However, the logic becomes a bit different because:

  • Each row generates its own instance of the field,
  • These controls appear dynamically as the table renders,
  • IDs are not static like a header field,
  • You must find each row’s field and attach the event individually,
  • You cannot rely on a fixed ID.

This makes the implementation slightly more complex, but still entirely doable.

entry.title: Re: Mapping custom retention field into the supplier invoice

entry.title: Re: Creating Simulation Template with start and end pages
entry.content:
hi 

✅ 1. Ensure the Start and End Pages themselves are published

SAP clarified that Start and End pages are separate content objects and must be explicitly published.
If they are not published individually, they will show up blank in Companion or other outputs.

“The Start and End Pages are separate content objects so must be published along with the Simulation.”
[community.sap.com]

✔ What to do:

  1. Go to the Book Page objects linked to the simulation.
  2. Right–click each Start Page and End Page.
  3. Select Publish.
  4. Re-publish the Simulation afterward.

✅ 2. Check whether the Template itself became corrupted

SAP notes that if the template used to generate Start/End pages is published, but pages appear blank, this could indicate a deeper issue.

“If the templates used to create the Start and End pages are published, please raise a support ticket… I cannot think of any other reason the page appears without content.”
[community.sap.com]

This aligns with your statement:

“It worked for a while… now all start and end pages appear blank.”

Templates can become corrupted especially if:

  • Styles or inherited objects were edited
  • The base template was renamed, moved, or unpublished
  • The linked pages were inadvertently modified

✅ 3. Test by creating a new simulation using the same template

This will reveal whether the template is the cause, not the individual simulations.

✔ Steps:

  1. Create a new blank simulation.
  2. Apply the same Simulation Template you previously used.
  3. Preview it in Producer and publish it.

🧪 Interpretation:

  • If Start/End pages are also blank → the template is broken.
  • If they appear normally → the issue is with your existing project objects.

✅ 4. If your template is corrupted, recreate it

You asked:

“If I have to create a new simulation template, how can I link it to existing simulations?”

Unfortunately, existing simulations can’t automatically swap to a new template.
You will need to manually insert the new Start/End pages into each affected simulation.

✔ To insert new pages:

  1. Open your simulation in Producer.
  2. Insert → Template Slide (the new Start or End Page).
    (SAP documentation confirms you can add template slides into already-created simulations.)
    [linkedin.com]
  3. Delete the blank/corrupted ones.
  4. Publish all objects again.

✅ 5. When all else fails: raise an SAP Support Ticket

If nothing fixes it:

"Please raise an SAP support ticket… use Component ID KM‑SEN‑MGR."
[community.sap.com]

 

Regards

Surjith

entry.title: Re: "Release of Purchase Requisition Item" not working sa...

entry.title: Re: Joule - Performance and Goals Agent
entry.content:
hi

Recommended Steps to Try Now

✔ 1. Confirm activation prerequisites

Ensure PM, GM, and/or CPM modules are actually active and contain data—not just “enabled.”

✔ 2. Re-check permissions

The manager must have the explicit permission:
AI Access → Performance & Goals Agent.

✔ 3. Manually trigger sync jobs

Run:

  • SF – Refresh Synthetic Group Data
  • IPS – User/Group Sync

This forces data to propagate to Joule.

✔ 4. Re-run CDM refresh

From SAP BTP Work Zone content manager.

✔ 5. Validate trusted domain setup

Misconfiguration can cause Joule to “see” no contextual data.

✔ 6. Test with multiple manager profiles

Sometimes individual users don’t have sufficient role exposure for Joule to understand their team hierarchy.

Regards

Surjith

entry.title: No access to EarlyWatch alerts for end customers
entry.content:
I should have access to EWA reports for all our customers; however, I can only view data for my own customer number.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No access to EarlyWatch alerts for end customers
entry.content:
In order for partner S-users to view end customers in the the Service Messages or SAP EarlyWatch Alert Workspace applications, their S-user ID needs to be given Service Reports (Partner) authorization. If a user is missing this authorization, use the self-service authorization request according to KBA 2891528 or contact an administrator listed on the My Important Contacts.
For further guidance, refer to the KBAs below: 

2929232 - SAP Partner cannot access end customer numbers in SAP EarlyWatch / Service Messages - SAP for Me
2627899 - How do VAR-delivered support partners check the EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) report for their end customers - SAP for Me

Thank you！
Yuko

entry.title: Re: Multiselect picklist dropdown

entry.title: How to add a Super Administrator
entry.content:
I would like to be assigned as the new super administrator for our company. Could you please provide instructions on how to proceed?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add a Super Administrator
entry.content:
As outlined in KBA 2596214 - How to maintain Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me
Customers are responsible for maintaining their own S-users and their own Administrators listed in the Important Contacts page as explained in KBA 1271517. For security reasons, SAP can only assist with adding an administrator when no administrators are currently available.
To add a Super Administrator, an existing Super Administrator must grant all necessary authorizations to another S-user ID. You can refer to KBA 1614304 for detailed instructions on how to copy or clone authorizations. I recommend contacting one of the existing Super Administrators listed in the https://me.sap.com/userscontacts/impcont to request the required authorization for your S-user ID

entry.title: Introducing Support for Different Time Granularities in Remote Models with Advanced Formulas
entry.content:
Introduction

As planning scenarios become increasingly complex, the need for flexible, high‑performance calculation logic has never been greater. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) continues to evolve to meet this demand, and the latest enhancements to Advanced Formulas introduce new levels of power and simplicity to model‑driven planning.

In this release, we are excited to introduce a key enhancement that streamlines common calculations, reduces script complexity, and expands what planners can achieve within the Data Action Script step. This blog highlights what's new, how it works, and how you can begin leveraging it in your planning models.

 

Support for Different Time-Granularity for Remote Models

One of the most impactful enhancements in this release is the new Support for Different Time‑Granularity for Remote Models.

Previously, remote models were constrained by the time granularity defined in the default model. This meant that the time granularity used in Advanced Formulas had to align precisely with both the source and the default model structure, often limiting planners when business requirements demanded more flexible or diverse time‑based calculations.

With this enhancement, Advanced Formulas now supports working seamlessly across multiple time granularities, even when retrieving data directly from remote models. This enables:

  • More flexible planning scenarios
  • Richer time‑series calculations
  • Better alignment between operational and financial planning processes
 

Key Benefits

More Natural Alignment with Business Cycles

Different planning functions operate at different rhythms. Financial planning typically uses monthly or quarterly cycles, while operational areas, such as Sales or Workforce, may rely heavily on weekly or daily views. Support for different time granularities allows remote models to reflect these variations naturally.

Enhanced Advanced Formula Logic

Advanced Formulas can now reference, calculate, and iterate across different time granularities without additional modeling workarounds. This significantly simplifies complex scripting and reduces the need for auxiliary aggregation models.

 

How It Works

This enhancement introduces the following capabilities:

  • Linked models can expose multiple levels of time granularity.
  • Advanced Formulas can reference time periods with higher or lower granularity than the default model.
  • The LINK() function now works reliably even when time granularities between models differ.

This ensures that time‑based calculations behave consistently across remote and planning models.

 

Practical Use Cases: Weekly Sales Data to Monthly Revenue Calculation

 

One powerful example of using this capability is referencing Weekly Sales data within a Monthly Financial Planning model. This ensures that planners maintain the precision of granular sales data while performing aggregated monthly revenue calculations.

 

Business Scenario Overview

Weekly Sales Model

  • Sales Quantity
  • Unit Price
  • Weekly date granularity

Monthly Financial Planning Model

  • Requires monthly Revenue values for forecasting and profitability analysis

 

Financial Model(Month)

 

Sales Model(Week)

 

Date Dimension(Week)

 

Source Data(Sales Model)

  • Weekly Total Revenue = Σ(Quantity × Price) = 1,000.00

 

Advanced Formulas Script

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202601" TO "202612"
MEMBERSET [d/Entity_D] = "US"
MEMBERSET [d/BusinessUnit] = "BU_SKU"
MEMBERSET [d/ProductGroup_D] = "SKU"

MODEL [Sales Planning]
	// Product_Item and Sales_Channel only exist in the linked source model Sales but not in default model Financial. They are used to define the source model scope.
	MEMBERSET [d/Product_Item] = ("SKU_001", "SKU_002", "SKU_003", "SKU_004", "SKU_005")
	MEMBERSET [d/Channel_Sales] = ("Direc", "Online", "Retail")
	//Since the Date dimension is not a shared dimension, you need to define the Date scope of the source model.
	MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202601" TO "202652"
ENDMODEL

// 33001000 stands for Account: Revenue Domestic-Product. 
// The granularities of the Date dimensions are different, adding syntax " [d/Date] = PERIOD([Sales Planning].[d/Date] " to accommodate for the difference.
DATA([d/Account_Fin] = "33001000", [d/DataSrc] = "DataSrc_Sales", [d/Date] = PERIOD([Sales Planning].[d/Date]))
= LINK([Sales Planning], [d/Measures] = "S90007000", [d/Version] = "public.Plan01")
* LINK([Sales Planning], [d/Measures] = "S90008000", [d/Version] = "public.Plan01")
  • When referencing data between models that use different time granularities (for example, weekly source → monthly target), you must explicitly align the time dimension in the DATA() statement. Use the PERIOD() function to map the source model’s time member to the target model’s time context.
     
    DATA(.., [d/Date] = PERIOD([Sales Planning].[d/Date]), ...) = LINK(...)

 

Result: Calculated Monthly Revenue

  • January (4 weeks): 4,000.00
  • February (4 weeks): 4,000.00
  • March (5 weeks): 5,000.00
     
 
 

Conclusion

This enhancement to time‑granularity handling significantly improves the flexibility and maintainability of planning scenarios in SAP Analytics Cloud. By enabling Advanced Formulas to reference weekly (or daily) data within a monthly model, organizations can plan more accurately and make better‑informed decisions.

We hope this new capability helps streamline your planning processes.
If you have feedback or want to share your own use cases, we invite you to join the conversation in the SAP Community.

 
Moonjun

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce SCIM integration with Okta
entry.content:
Hello
Generally, from commerce cloud perspective , the key point is get the Commerce server to accept a 3rd party IDP.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/aa417173fe4a4ba5a473c93eb730a417/633fecd6e762440e850978cdc57afc10.html

There was some document which provide the sample regarding integrate OKTA with commerce cloud: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/single-sign-on-sap-commerce-cloud-hybris-pushpak-agarwal/

However, integrate OKTA via cloudscimwebservices seems rare use case, maybe you can share some more details regarding business requirement and let us know the background?
Best regards,

Kevin

entry.title: G/L account for Vietnam rollout implementation
entry.content:
Hello All, 
I am configuring the Vietnam G/L accounts, and wanted to make sure that my procedure is correct. 


1. I have obtained a list of G/L accounts according to the Vietnamese Accounting Standards

2. I manually added the list into the YAVN chart of accounts. The default account codes in YAVN does not match those of the accounting standards. 

3. Map the YAVN codes to YCOA in the alternative g/l account section. Also, what should I do in the case where none of the YCOA is a good mapping target to the YAVN account?

 

Best,

entry.title: Re: SAP Project Systems -BAPIs (A quick overview)
entry.content:
@Abhishek_Band  - There are no dedicated standard BAPIs specifically designed for reading overall, annual current budget, budget released, and budget assigned values in SAP Project Systems. However, there are alternative approaches available.

Alternative Approaches

Function Modules

While specific BAPIs are not available, you can explore these alternatives:

  • Function Module Approach: Search for function modules in SE80 or SE37 using patterns like: BAPI_PROJECT_* , *BUDGET* ,*PS_*

  • Custom Development Approach

    Since standard BAPIs are not available, consider creating a custom function module with a developer

entry.title: Re: Adobe live Cycle Designer 11. PL25 - SAP GUI 770 : Er...
entry.content:
If you log on EN language, the issue is solved.  You can edit your SFP form if you log in EN. other language error SOFFICEINTEGRATION 143.

other way is delete temporal files of adobe live cicle designer, but is unconfortable to have to delete files to edit your form.

There is other solution it is the easyest. 

Kind regards,

entry.title: Standard Workflows in SAP PPM
entry.content:
Core Workflow Categories

Project Management Workflows
  • Project status change workflows for tasks, checklist items, phases, and projects.
  • Project element approval workflows triggered when project leaders release elements ,
  • Change management workflows for network modifications and configuration changes ,
Portfolio Management Workflows
  • Item deletion workflow (WS00800003)
  • Item attribute change workflow (WS00800004)
  • Item status change workflow (WS00800008)
  • Item decision point workflows for status and attribute changes
Resource Management Workflows
  • Purchase order change workflow (TS20000653) for material components and external processing 
  • Confirmation workflows for entering actual data (TS00007944) 
  • Deviation workflows triggered when duration or work exceeds predefined values 
Financial Management Workflows
  • Initiative deletion workflow (WS00700032)
  • Initiative attribute change workflow (WS00700022)
  • Initiative status change workflow (WS00700021)
  • Initiative decision point workflows for approvals and changes

Workflow Details

Item Status Change Workflow (WS00800008)
  • Purpose: Automates notifications when portfolio item status changes occur.
  • Trigger: Status modification of portfolio items
  • Steps: Status validation, recipient determination, notification generation
  • Participants: Item owners, approvers, and stakeholders with relevant authorizations
  • Outcome: Automated email notifications with links to affected portfolio items
Configuration Change Management Workflow (WS20000265)
  • Purpose: Manages configuration changes in sales orders with network conflicts 
  • Trigger: Configuration change creating manual adjustment requirements 
  • Steps: Display change management, create text, display text, make change 
  • Participants: Dispatcher, project planning employees, MRP controllers
  • Outcome: Systematic resolution of configuration conflicts ,
Purchase Order Change Workflow (TS20000653)
  • Purpose: Handles changes to purchase orders for material components 
  • Trigger: Modifications to dates, quantities, customers, vendors, or addresses in networks 
  • Steps: Change detection, notification to purchasing, direct purchase order modification 
  • Participants: Purchasing department personnel 
  • Outcome: Updated purchase orders reflecting project changes 

Configuration Considerations

  • Workflow activation through PPM Customizing under Portfolio Management → Global Customizing → Global Settings → Workflow Settings
  • BAdI implementation activation for attribute change workflows (RPM_PROJ_CUST_WF for items, /RPM/DES_POINT_WF for decision points)
  • Web server alias configuration for portal links in notifications
  • Standard task assignment to authorized agents and event link activation 

Best Practices

  • Configure workflow recipients based on authorization activities (admin, read, write) to ensure proper notification distribution 
  • Ensure user email addresses are properly maintained for successful notification delivery
  • Use standard workflow IDs provided by SAP before creating custom workflows 
  • Monitor workflow performance using transaction SBWP to track started workflows 
  • Leverage function group /RPM/WORKFLOW for debugging and troubleshooting workflow issues

entry.title: Re: "Error while opening document" in Form Builder - usin...
entry.content:

Miguel Ángel Orihuela.

entry.title: Re: SD Condition Based Down Payments
entry.content:
Very nice sharing,

But I still have a confusion, how to make sure that the SO is blocked when we have not received the down payment of customer.

entry.title: Re: Contingent Worker/External to Employee Conversions th...
entry.content:
@tracymca 

One approach that can be followed is: 

  1. As an active or terminated Contingent Worker, the applicant can be added to Onboarding either through manual Onboarding, Recruiting, or external ATS systems and follows the standard Onboarding process.
  2. User receives email with login credentials for the future employment and performs Personal Data Collection.
  3. After Personal Data Collection, the 2nd Rehire check will initiate. This action will trigger because enough rehire match data has been identified.
  4. Within the rehire verification match, all inactive, active employees and active employees will future dated terminations who match the rehire criteria will appear in the rehire verification step.
  5. After the user has been identified as a Rehire on New Employment, the user can login via existing login credentials from the active Contingent Employment, or new login credentials for the future employment.
  6. The user can switch between the current (Active) and future (Inactive) profiles through the Context Switcher to complete necessary tasks.
  7. The user must be on the Inactive profile in order to complete the remaining Onboarding tasks (Rehire Data Review, Compliance forms, etc.)

Additional Information:

  • This includes support of a time gap between the termination date and start date (ie: Contract ends on Friday and full time position starts Monday).
  • If the contingent worker’s contract end date has not been entered, this will need to be completed in Employee Central, before the rehire verification can be completed.
    • To enter the contract end date, navigate to Employee Central and update the date, once the date has been entered, the rehire verification step will reflect the update and allow for action to be taken.
  • Rehire with New Employment button will be available for selection allowing the contingent worker to be converted to a regular employee and the rest of the onboarding process flow will continue.

KBA reference - 3125911 - How to Hire a Contingent Worker - Onboarding - SAP for Me

I hope this helps!

 

Regards,

Tabassum

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026
entry.content:
Hello @DianaRoesner, I’ve since retaken the End-to-End Business Processes practical exam and also attempted the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Financial Accounting practical exam.

Unfortunately, in both cases I encountered system issues that prevented progression beyond the second task. Support tickets were logged during the exam window, but the issues could not be resolved before time elapsed.

As a result, both exams are now recorded as failed and two exam attempt credits have been consumed, despite the failures being caused by unresolved system defects rather than candidate performance.

I’m raising this to seek guidance on the appropriate remediation path (recredit and invalidation of attempts) and to understand how candidates should proceed when system blockers occur during active practical exams.

entry.title: Re: Is freight order confirmation Mandatory for Freight T...
entry.content: Great thanks for your kind and detailed clarification. Very clear now. Thanks again.

entry.title: Re: Triggering Australian Compliance Forms for New Hires...
entry.content:
Hi @karunakarsadu ,

You can use FieldMetaOverrideConfiguration to override configuration in global filter used for assignment of compliance forms to the user.

You can create a custom field to store the Home country of the candidate and set the entity path accordingly. 

Please ensure you are selecting the appropriate meta override type: 

Global Filter Metadata Override - This meta override type is used to assign compliance forms to an address type other than the default address type.

Form Metadata Override - This meta override type is used to override the new hire personal data reporting in form. Enter EC_META_CONFIG as the ID for the configuration to be global and applicable to all forms. When using this ID, you can leave the supportedTypes field blank.

Guide Link - Setting Up Compliance Form Assignment Using Override Configuration | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026
entry.content: Excellent

entry.title: Re: Business data validation using DTV tool
entry.content:
Hi @BiankaK 

Sorry for replying late to this message, hope the issue would have been resolved by now, if not please create ticket using component BC-UPG-DVF.

Thanks, and Regards

Abhya

entry.title: Re: Paying with stablecoins in B2B scenarios - business process end-to-end
entry.content:
Wonderful eye opener Thomas, i believe it is about WHEN and not IF.

We should soon see a proliferation of B2B STABLECOIN payments especially for Cross Border payments as it is almost instant and reduces the payment charges substantially. 

Also with unique wallet address for each of business partner, cash application becomes so much simpler. Will need further support from Government and trust from Businesses. SAP is placed in a strategic position to accelerate this adoption with so many of their customers on their platform

entry.title: Roadmap Inquiry: UI5 Table Grouping Expand/Collapse for OData V4
entry.content:
Hi,
Our SBN team has encountered an issue during the development of the table group function for the catalog. The data volume of our catalog table will be extremely large, and we need to group the data by customer. However, there is currently no available control to support this functionality.
 
We would like to inquire: When does our UI5 control library plan to support the expand/collapse functionality for grouping?
 
The Analytical Table can fulfill the requirement, but it does not support OData V4. We will not revert to the older OData V2 protocol just to compensate for this missing frontend feature.
Meanwhile, the Tree Table is not suitable for our use case.
As a result, we currently do not have an appropriate control to support expand and collapse for group functionality.
 
The Fiori Elements Framework ALV supports the latest OData V4, but migrating to this solution involves cost.
Therefore, we would like to know if there is an upgrade roadmap for the UI5 Table group expand/collapse function.
 
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Distribution of Freight Cost for Pre-Carriage
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Requesting your support for the below scenario.

I am trying to create a freight order for pick up (in case of export freight booking) freight order for delivery (in case of import). To create this type of freight orders I am selecting Container from Items tab and Create Freight Order from same item tab. In this case my cost distribution does not work as in my distribution profile cost is distributed on Weight and weight is not copied from my Freight Booking and thus giving me error "Freight Settlement Document is Inconsistent".

If I select Freight Unit instead of container from same Items tab and create FO for Pick up/Delivery; my cost distribution works but my freight document could have multiple freight units and selecting just one of those to create Pick Up/Delivery FO could be wrong practice.

I am using hierarchical distribution in my profile

SAP Transportation Management

We are thrilled to extend a warm welcome to everyone for our SAP Cloud ALM technical and configuration deep dive webinar series.

  • Q&A - 5 mins

We look forward to having you attend the event!

entry.title: "Hide UserName in the UI" for Onboarding 2.0- Is this Mandatory to be disabled?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone-

Is it mandatory to disable "Hide UserName inHide Username in the UI" in Platform Feature Settings for Initiating Onboarding Manually without using Recruiting.  The documentation given by SAP says to do.

My customer has enabled this setting , Would this be a major impact on EC or Onboarding?.  Please advice.

Link- https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-onboarding/implementing-onboarding/initiating-onboarding-process-manually?q=hide+username

entry.title: Transitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Selective Data Transfer for SAP Cloud ALM (3/5) (EMEA)
entry.content:
Transitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Selective Data Transfer for SAP Cloud ALM (3/5) (EMEA)

You’ve been invited to:


SAP Cloud ALM Technical and Configuration Deep Dive 2026 

We are thrilled to extend a warm welcome to everyone for our SAP Cloud ALM technical and configuration deep dive webinar series.

Topic

 

Transitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Selective Data Transfer for SAP Cloud ALM (3/5) (EMEA)

 

Agenda

  • SAP Cloud ALM Overview 
  • How to migrate your Process Hierarchy and Libraries form SAP Solution Manager
  • How to transition test management from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
  • Gain best practices for consolidating you solution documentation and move it to Cloud ALM
  • Q&A

See you there!

Registration Link - Click here

We look forward to having you attend the event!

entry.title: Feature Activation Hub in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Introduction

With Service/Sales Cloud V2, SAP now offers a capability called Feature Activation Hub, which allows you as an administrator to view and activate new features, before they are made generally available.

We will explore this new capability in this blog.

Access Feature activation Hub

     To access the Feature Activation Hub, you need to add the required Service & App to your business role as shown below:

  1. Go to System Settings  -> Users and Control  - > Roles
     

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

        2.  Add the business service CustomerFeatureToggleService to your administrator business role.

     3. Enable  Customer Features  app.

Activate New Features

   You can now  view and activate new features before the features are activated by default.

  1.   Go to System Settings  -> System Preferences  - > Feature Activation Hub

    2. You can view the details of the new features which are available for activation before they are activated by default in all systems.

Let's look at the different options on activation page:

       A.  Quick filter by Available Date & Service.

       B.  Explore all new features in Help Portal.

      C. Sort by Available Date, Activation date & Service

      D.  Get quick information about the specific feature, you can click on See More to get more details in What's New viewer on the SAP Help Portal.

    

E.  Once you have enough information and you think the new feature will help your organization boost productivity, you can activate the new feature by selecting the Activate checkbox.

Important information

  • Once you activate a feature, you can't deactivate it. It is  recommended that you activate new features in your test or development tenants before activating them in your production tenant.
  • New features listed in Feature Activation Hub are activated by default after the stated period of time. You can only opt-in early if you want.

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite in 2025 & 2026

entry.title: Remove old PID / unlink old email from SAP Account Manager
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support team,
I had emailed earlier regarding an old PID user that I would like to remove.

I need that old PID/email **fully removed or unlinked** from my SAP Universal ID/Account Manager so that it no longer appears.
Do I have the chance to unlink or remove the old P-user from the UID by my own? Or will you help and unlink the user from my UID?
Please assist with removing the old PID/email from my account. Let me know if any further details or verification are needed.
Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Remove old PID / unlink old email from SAP Account Ma...
entry.content:
Hello customer,

Yes now customer is able to unlink user(s) from the UID on customer side via self-service.

Kindly refer to the detailed steps documented in KBA 3619621 - How to unlink a user from an SAP Universal ID in SAP Account Manager?

But kindly know users cannot delete an SAP Universal ID directly through Self-Service. Please submit a case under component XX-SER-UID to request a UID completely deleted on SAP side. Refer to KBA 3539768.

In case you encounter any issue while unlinking user from UID, also feel free to submit a case under XX-SER-UID with the issue details.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: After initiating ONB, if we find wrong mail ID how can we correct to trigger the email notification
entry.content:
Hello Team,

During the onboarding if the onboardee enters wrong email id how can we correct the same on the SAP success factors

After initiating ONB, if we find wrong mail ID how can we correct to trigger the email notification.

Can anybody give any solution to the above issue.

entry.title: Preparation for ePPDS certification with SAP learning hub
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to learn ePPDS. I have recently received SAP learing hub access and want to use the cources available to learn ePPDS. Can someone suggest me the best available cources in learning hub?
I have a C_TS422_2022 certification. Is there any separate certification available specific to ePPDS?

Thank you,

entry.title: Adding filter to a measure variable
entry.content: Hey, I'm trying to add a filter to a measure variable created which is like a remark on whether to keep listed, delist or review a product. When I apply a filter I'm unable to filter it based on these values of keep listed, delist, or review product. Could anyone help me out please
